Container.Name: UnrarTool
Container.Overview:
UnrarTool — a sleek self-hosted RAR extraction tool built for Unraid.
Automatically detects and extracts split RAR archives (RAR5, multi-part .partNN.rar and legacy .r00/.r01 formats) downloaded by Sonarr, Radarr, or any other downloader.
Features: ▸ File Browser with filter, sort, and multi-select ▸ Real-time progress bar driven directly from unrar output ▸ Watch Folders + Scheduler for automatic extraction ▸ Sonarr / Radarr / Lidarr / Readarr webhook integration ▸ Smart exclusions — never re-extracts the same archive twice ▸ Clean Up tool to remove extracted files after import ▸ Coloured source badges so you always know what triggered an extraction ▸ Dark / Light theme
Container.Description: UnrarTool — Automated split-RAR extractor with a modern web UI. Real-time progress, smart exclusions, watch folders, webhook integration, and a clean-up tool. Built for Sonarr/Radarr workflows on Unraid.
Container.Changes:
### 1.4.3 - Fix: removed dead PORT environment variable that duplicated the port mapping field - Fix: removed editable DATA_PATH/CONFIG_PATH variables — always required to be /data and /config, now fixed internally with nothing to configure - Note: if you see Python-internal variables (LANG, GPG_KEY, PYTHON_VERSION) in your template, remove and re-add the container using the Template URL above — those came from a raw image inspection, not this template
### 1.4.2 - Fix: removed hardcoded /data/media volume that was causing Unraid to auto-create an empty "media" share on servers that don't have that path
### 1.4.1 - Fix: webhook path resolution now uses downloadFolder first - Fix: broadened defer detection for mid-download extraction attempts
### 1.4.0 - Clean Up feature with file tracking and live deletion progress
### 1.3.x - Sources page, coloured source badges, dashboard widget - 4-state webhook status indicator - Auto-defer for mid-download files
### 1.0.0 - Initial release

Config.Name: Web UI Port
Config.Description: Port for the UnrarTool web interface. Change the left number to use a different host port if 8080 is taken.

Config.Name: Config and Database
Config.Description: Stores the database, settings, and trash folder. Must be persistent.

Config.Name: Downloads Folder
Config.Description: Your downloads folder. This is the root shown in the file browser. To add more paths (e.g. a second drive), click Add another Path in the Unraid Docker settings and mount them under /data/whatever.

Config.Name: PUID
Config.Description: User ID for file ownership. Unraid default is 99 (nobody).

Config.Name: PGID
Config.Description: Group ID for file ownership. Unraid default is 100 (users).
